---
title: "R Workflows"
output: html_document
---

## Appendix 3 - R Workflows and GitHub template

The files generated in the [<u>EventMeasure Exports</u>](#appendix-2---how-to-download-and-summarise-annotation-data) section of this manual are the input files required for the [<u>Synthesi</u>](#synthesis)s template, which can be accessed [<u>here</u>](https://github.com/UWA-Marine-Ecology-Group-syntheses/template-synthesis). Running the [<u>Synthesis</u>](#synthesis) script produces the output files that are ready to be uploaded to the [<u>Synthesis</u>](#synthesis) on GlobalArchive.

- Below is an example of the files that will be generated from this script.

- This script can be used the combine the data of multiple [<u>Campaigns</u>](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yU-zrEIwBN1B-w-rnwxRlZ8rEioJ37bxk7l5LkXhgoM/edit?userstoinvite=annika.leunig%40marineecology.io&sharingaction=manageaccess&role=reader&tab=t.0#heading=h.watfbpgrrufl) into 1 file<img src="images/image11.png" style="width:6.25in;height:0.76042in" />

> More handy R workflows coming soon…

##
